summary: hyper potions lava chicken music doesn t play
description: i played the lava chicken music disc on a jukebox, it plays but there is no audio, all of the volumes in the Music amp; Sounds tab is all set to 100 , although, this ever happened with Amos Roddy - Tears but it got fixed in the 1.21.6 release, but this time, it didn t get fixed in the 1.21.7 release
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Start Minecraft and create a new Creative world with default settings.
2. Obtain the "Lava Chicken" music disc from your inventory or by using the command `/give @p minecraft:music_disc_lava_chicken`.
3. Place a Jukebox on the ground by selecting it from your inventory and right-clicking on the ground.
4. Insert the "Lava Chicken" music disc into the Jukebox by right-clicking on the Jukebox while holding the disc.
5. Ensure that all volume settings in the Music & Sounds tab are set to 100% by navigating to Options > Music & Sounds.
6. Observe that the Jukebox appears to play the music disc (indicated by the spinning disc and note particles), but no audio is produced.
7. Test with other music discs, specifically the "Amos Roddy - Tears" disc, to verify that the issue is specific to the "Lava Chicken" disc.
8. Confirm that the issue persists after updating to version 1.21.7 and does not occur in version 1.21.6.
Observed Behavior:
When the "Lava Chicken" music disc is played in a jukebox, the disc appears to play as indicated by the visual cue (note particles emitting from the top of the jukebox). However, no audio is produced, rendering the music track silent. This issue persists despite all audio settings in the Music & Sounds menu being set to 100%, confirming that the problem is not related to player volume settings. 

This bug is similar to a previous issue with the "Amos Roddy - Tears" music disc, which was resolved in version 1.21.6. However, the "Lava Chicken" music disc remains unresponsive in terms of audio output even after the 1.21.7 update, indicating that it has not been addressed in the latest release. 

To replicate the issue:
1. Obtain the "Lava Chicken" music disc.
2. Place a jukebox in the game world.
3. Insert the "Lava Chicken" music disc into the jukebox.
4. Observe that the jukebox indicates playback but produces no sound.

This behavior disrupts the intended experience of enjoying the music associated with the disc and requires attention for resolution.
